preamble: A shift from the tip of the tongue (dental/alveolar) to the back (velar) is relatively rare but well-documented in specific environments, often driven by place assimilation or specific historical sound laws.

examples.note: Proto-Athabaskan 'wife': syllable-final /d/ → /g/ in Upper Umpqua. Could not verify against a published source; flagged.
Proto-Athabaskan 'smoke': same coda shift. Could not verify against a published source; flagged.
Alveolar /d/ → velar /g/ in Shambaa adaptation of Swahili loanword. Could not verify in published sources; flagged.
